NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. On an instrument of Gath. A Psalm of Asaph. Sing aloud to God our strength; Make a joyful shout to the God of Jacob.
NKJV.2: Raise a song and strike the timbrel, The pleasant harp with the lute.
NKJV.3: Blow the trumpet at the time of the New Moon, At the full moon, on our solemn feast day.
NKJV.4: For this is a statute for Israel, A law of the God of Jacob.
NKJV.5: This He established in Joseph as a testimony, When He went throughout the land of Egypt, Where I heard a language I did not understand.
NKJV.6: “I removed his shoulder from the burden; His hands were freed from the baskets.
NKJV.7: You called in trouble, and I delivered you; I answered you in the secret place of thunder; I tested you at the waters of Meribah. Selah
NKJV.8: “Hear,  O My people, and I will admonish you! O Israel, if you will listen to Me!
NKJV.9: There shall be no foreign god among you; Nor shall you worship any foreign god.
NKJV.10: I am the LORD your God, Who brought you out of the land of Egypt; Open your mouth wide, and I will fill it.
NKJV.11: “But My people would not heed My voice, And Israel would have none of Me.
NKJV.12: So I gave them over to their own stubborn heart, To walk in their own counsels.
NKJV.13: “Oh,  that My people would listen to Me, That Israel would walk in My ways!
NKJV.14: I would soon subdue their enemies, And turn My hand against their adversaries.
NKJV.15: The haters of the LORD would pretend submission to Him, But their fate would endure forever.
NKJV.16: He would have fed them also with the finest of wheat; And with honey from the rock I would have satisfied you.”

KJV.1: To the chief Musician<S>5329</S> upon Gittith<S>1665</S>, A Psalm of Asaph<S>623</S>. Sing aloud<S>7442</S> unto God<S>430</S> our strength<S>5797</S>: make a joyful noise<S>7321</S> unto the God<S>430</S> of Jacob<S>3290</S>. <sup>of Asaph: or, for Asaph</sup>
KJV.2: Take<S>5375</S> a psalm<S>2172</S>, and bring<S>5414</S> hither the timbrel<S>8596</S>, the pleasant<S>5273</S> harp<S>3658</S> with the psaltery<S>5035</S>.
KJV.3: Blow up<S>8628</S> the trumpet<S>7782</S> in the new moon<S>2320</S>, in the time appointed<S>3677</S>, on our solemn feast<S>2282</S> day<S>3117</S>.
KJV.4: For this was a statute<S>2706</S> for Israel<S>3478</S>, and a law<S>4941</S> of the God<S>430</S> of Jacob<S>3290</S>.
KJV.5: This he ordained<S>7760</S> in Joseph<S>3084</S> for a testimony<S>5715</S>, when he went out<S>3318</S> through the land<S>776</S> of Egypt<S>4714</S>: where I heard<S>8085</S> a language<S>8193</S> that I understood<S>3045</S> not. <sup>through: or, against</sup>
KJV.6: I removed<S>5493</S> his shoulder<S>7926</S> from the burden<S>5447</S>: his hands<S>3709</S> were delivered<S>5674</S> from the pots<S>1731</S>. <sup>were: Heb. passed away</sup>
KJV.7: Thou calledst<S>7121</S> in trouble<S>6869</S>, and I delivered<S>2502</S> thee; I answered<S>6030</S> thee in the secret place<S>5643</S> of thunder<S>7482</S>: I proved<S>974</S> thee at the waters<S>4325</S> of Meribah<S>4809</S>. Selah<S>5542</S>. <sup>Meribah: or, Strife</sup>
KJV.8: Hear<S>8085</S>, O my people<S>5971</S>, and I will testify<S>5749</S> unto thee: O Israel<S>3478</S>, if thou wilt hearken<S>8085</S> unto me;
KJV.9: There shall no strange<S>2114</S> god<S>410</S> be in thee; neither shalt thou worship<S>7812</S> any strange<S>5236</S> god<S>410</S>.
KJV.10: I am the LORD<S>3068</S> thy God<S>430</S>, which brought<S>5927</S> thee out of the land<S>776</S> of Egypt<S>4714</S>: open thy mouth<S>6310</S> wide<S>7337</S>, and I will fill<S>4390</S> it.
KJV.11: But my people<S>5971</S> would not hearken<S>8085</S> to my voice<S>6963</S>; and Israel<S>3478</S> would<S>14</S> none of me.
KJV.12: So I gave them up<S>7971</S> unto their own hearts'<S>3820</S> lust<S>8307</S>: and they walked<S>3212</S> in their own counsels<S>4156</S>. <sup>unto: or, to the hardness of their hearts, or, imagination</sup>
KJV.13: Oh<S>3863</S> that my people<S>5971</S> had hearkened<S>8085</S> unto me, and Israel<S>3478</S> had walked<S>1980</S> in my ways<S>1870</S>!
KJV.14: I should soon<S>4592</S> have subdued<S>3665</S> their enemies<S>341</S>, and turned<S>7725</S> my hand<S>3027</S> against their adversaries<S>6862</S>.
KJV.15: The haters<S>8130</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S> should have submitted<S>3584</S> themselves unto him: but their time<S>6256</S> should have endured for ever<S>5769</S>. <sup>submitted: or, yielded feigned obedience: Heb. lied</sup>
KJV.16: He should have fed<S>398</S> them also with the finest<S>2459</S> of the wheat<S>2406</S>: and with honey<S>1706</S> out of the rock<S>6697</S> should I have satisfied<S>7646</S> thee. <sup>finest: Heb. fat of wheat</sup>

ESV.1: Sing aloud to God our strength; shout for joy to the God of Jacob!
ESV.2: Raise a song; sound the tambourine, the sweet lyre with the harp.
ESV.3: Blow the trumpet at the new moon, at the full moon, on our feast day.
ESV.4: For it is a statute for Israel, a rule  of the God of Jacob.
ESV.5: He made it a decree in Joseph when he went out over  the land of Egypt. I hear a language I had not known:
ESV.6: “I relieved your  shoulder of the burden; your hands were freed from the basket.
ESV.7: In distress you called, and I delivered you; I answered you in the secret place of thunder; I tested you at the waters of Meribah. Selah
ESV.8: Hear, O my people, while I admonish you! O Israel, if you would but listen to me!
ESV.9: There shall be no strange god among you; you shall not bow down to a foreign god.
ESV.10: I am the LORD your God, who brought you up out of the land of Egypt. Open your mouth wide, and I will fill it.
ESV.11: “But my people did not listen to my voice; Israel would not submit to me.
ESV.12: So I gave them over to their stubborn hearts, to follow their own counsels.
ESV.13: Oh, that my people would listen to me, that Israel would walk in my ways!
ESV.14: I would soon subdue their enemies and turn my hand against their foes.
ESV.15: Those who hate the LORD would cringe toward him, and their fate would last forever.
ESV.16: But he would feed you  with the finest of the wheat, and with honey from the rock I would satisfy you.”

NASB.1: For the choir director; on the Gittith. [A Psalm] of Asaph. Sing for joy to God our strength; Shout joyfully to the God of Jacob.
NASB.2: Raise a song, strike the timbrel, The sweet sounding lyre with the harp.
NASB.3: Blow the trumpet at the new moon, At the full moon, on our feast day.
NASB.4: For it is a statute for Israel, An ordinance of the God of Jacob.
NASB.5: He established it for a testimony in Joseph When he went throughout the land of Egypt. I heard a language that I did not know:
NASB.6: 'I relieved his shoulder of the burden, His hands were freed from the basket.
NASB.7: 'You called in trouble and I rescued you; I answered you in the hiding place of thunder; I proved you at the waters of Meribah. Selah.
NASB.8: 'Hear, O My people, and I will admonish you; O Israel, if you would listen to Me!
NASB.9: 'Let there be no strange god among you; Nor shall you worship any foreign god.
NASB.10: 'I, the LORD, am your God, Who brought you up from the land of Egypt; Open your mouth wide and I will fill it.
NASB.11: 'But My people did not listen to My voice, And Israel did not obey Me.
NASB.12: 'So I gave them over to the stubbornness of their heart, To walk in their own devices.
NASB.13: 'Oh that My people would listen to Me, That Israel would walk in My ways!
NASB.14: 'I would quickly subdue their enemies And turn My hand against their adversaries.
NASB.15: 'Those who hate the LORD would pretend obedience to Him, And their time [of punishment] would be forever.
NASB.16: 'But I would feed you with the finest of the wheat, And with honey from the rock I would satisfy you.'

NLT.1:
Sing praises to God, our strength.
Sing to the God of Jacob.
NLT.2:
Sing! Beat the tambourine.
Play the sweet lyre and the harp.
NLT.3:
Blow the ram’s horn at new moon,
and again at full moon to call a festival!
NLT.4:
For this is required by the decrees of Israel;
it is a regulation of the God of Jacob.
NLT.5:
He made it a law for Israel
when he attacked Egypt to set us free.
I heard an unknown voice say,
NLT.6:
“Now I will take the load from your shoulders;
I will free your hands from their heavy tasks.
NLT.7:
You cried to me in trouble, and I saved you;
I answered out of the thundercloud
and tested your faith when there was no water at Meribah. Interlude
NLT.8:
“Listen to me, O my people, while I give you stern warnings.
O Israel, if you would only listen to me!
NLT.9:
You must never have a foreign god;
you must not bow down before a false god.
NLT.10:
For it was I, the LORD your God,
who rescued you from the land of Egypt.
Open your mouth wide, and I will fill it with good things.
NLT.11:
“But no, my people wouldn’t listen.
Israel did not want me around.
NLT.12:
So I let them follow their own stubborn desires,
living according to their own ideas.
NLT.13:
Oh, that my people would listen to me!
Oh, that Israel would follow me, walking in my paths!
NLT.14:
How quickly I would then subdue their enemies!
How soon my hands would be upon their foes!
NLT.15:
Those who hate the LORD would cringe before him;
they would be doomed forever.
NLT.16:
But I would feed you with the finest wheat.
I would satisfy you with wild honey from the rock.”

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician. On an instrument of Gath. By Asaph. Sing aloud to God, our strength! Make a joyful shout to the God of Jacob!
WEB.2: Raise a song, and bring here the tambourine, the pleasant lyre with the harp.
WEB.3: Blow the trumpet at the New Moon, at the full moon, on our feast day.
WEB.4: For it is a statute for Israel, an ordinance of the God of Jacob.
WEB.5: He appointed it in Joseph for a testimony, when he went out over the land of Egypt, I heard a language that I didn’t know.
WEB.6: “I removed his shoulder from the burden. His hands were freed from the basket.
WEB.7: You called in trouble, and I delivered you. I answered you in the secret place of thunder. I tested you at the waters of Meribah.” Selah.
WEB.8: “Hear, my people, and I will testify to you, Israel, if you would listen to me!
WEB.9: There shall be no strange god in you, neither shall you worship any foreign god.
WEB.10: I am Yahweh, your God, who brought you up out of the land of Egypt. Open your mouth wide, and I will fill it.
WEB.11: But my people didn’t listen to my voice. Israel desired none of me.
WEB.12: So I let them go after the stubbornness of their hearts, that they might walk in their own counsels.
WEB.13: Oh that my people would listen to me, that Israel would walk in my ways!
WEB.14: I would soon subdue their enemies, and turn my hand against their adversaries.
WEB.15: The haters of Yahweh would cringe before him, and their punishment would last forever.
WEB.16: But he would have also fed them with the finest of the wheat. I will satisfy you with honey out of the rock.”

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician; set to the Philistine lute. A Psalm of Asaph. Sing aloud to God our strength;
Shout for joy to the God of Jacob (Israel).
AMP.2:
Raise a song, sound the timbrel,
The sweet sounding lyre with the harp.
AMP.3:
Blow the trumpet at the New Moon,
At the full moon, on our feast day.
AMP.4:
For this is a statute for Israel,
An ordinance of the God of Jacob.
AMP.5:
He established it for a testimony in Joseph
When He went throughout the land of Egypt.
I heard the language [of One whom] I did not know, saying,
AMP.6:
"I removed the burden from his shoulder;
His hands were freed from the basket.
AMP.7:
"You called in [the time of] trouble and I rescued you;
I answered you in the secret place of thunder;
I tested you at the waters of Meribah. Selah.
AMP.8:
"Hear, O My people, and I will admonish you—
O Israel, if you would listen to Me!
AMP.9:
"Let there be no strange god among you,
Nor shall you worship any foreign god.
AMP.10:
"I am the LORD your God,
Who brought you up from the land of Egypt.
Open your mouth wide and I will fill it.
AMP.11:
"But My people would not listen to My voice,
And Israel did not [consent to] obey Me.
AMP.12:
"So I gave them up to the stubbornness of their heart,
To walk in [the path of] their own counsel.
AMP.13:
"Oh, that My people would listen to Me,
That Israel would walk in My ways!
AMP.14:
"Then I would quickly subdue and humble their enemies
And turn My hand against their adversaries;
AMP.15:
Those who hate the LORD would pretend obedience to Him and cringe before Him,
And their time of punishment would be forever.
AMP.16:
"But I would feed Israel with the finest of the wheat;
And with honey from the rock I would satisfy you."

MSG.1:
An Asaph psalm
A song to our strong God!
a shout to the God of Jacob!
MSG.2:
Anthems from the choir, music from the band,
sweet sounds from lute and harp,
MSG.3:
Trumpets and trombones and horns:
it's festival day, a feast to God!
MSG.4:
A day decreed by God,
solemnly ordered by the God of Jacob.
MSG.5:
He commanded Joseph to keep this day
so we'd never forget what he did in Egypt.
I hear this most gentle whisper from One
I never guessed would speak to me:
MSG.6:
"I took the world off your shoulders,
freed you from a life of hard labor.
MSG.7:
You called to me in your pain;
I got you out of a bad place.
I answered you from where the thunder hides,
I proved you at Meribah Fountain.
MSG.8:
"Listen, dear ones—get this straight;
O Israel, don't take this lightly.
MSG.9:
Don't take up with strange gods,
don't worship the latest in gods.
MSG.10:
I'm God, your God, the very God
who rescued you from doom in Egypt,
Then fed you all you could eat,
filled your hungry stomachs.
MSG.11:
"But my people didn't listen,
Israel paid no attention;
MSG.12:
So I let go of the reins and told them, 'Run!
Do it your own way!'
MSG.13:
"Oh, dear people, will you listen to me now?
Israel, will you follow my map?
MSG.14:
I'll make short work of your enemies,
give your foes the back of my hand.
MSG.15:
I'll send the God-haters cringing like dogs,
never to be heard from again.
MSG.16:
You'll feast on my fresh-baked bread
spread with butter and rock-pure honey."

NIV.1:
Psalm 81
For the director of music. According to gittith. Of Asaph.
Sing for joy to God our strength;
shout aloud to the God of Jacob!
NIV.2:
Begin the music, strike the tambourine,
play the melodious harp and lyre.
NIV.3:
Sound the ram’s horn at the New Moon,
and when the moon is full, on the day of our Feast;
NIV.4:
this is a decree for Israel,
an ordinance of the God of Jacob.
NIV.5:
He established it as a statute for Joseph
when he went out against Egypt,
where we heard a language we did not understand.
NIV.6:
He says, “I removed the burden from their shoulders;
their hands were set free from the basket.
NIV.7:
In your distress you called and I rescued you,
I answered you out of a thundercloud;
I tested you at the waters of Meribah. Selah
NIV.8:
“Hear, O my people, and I will warn you—
if you would but listen to me, O Israel!
NIV.9:
You shall have no foreign god among you;
you shall not bow down to an alien god.
NIV.10:
I am the Lord your God,
who brought you up out of Egypt.
Open wide your mouth and I will fill it.
NIV.11:
“But my people would not listen to me;
Israel would not submit to me.
NIV.12:
So I gave them over to their stubborn hearts
to follow their own devices.
NIV.13:
“If my people would but listen to me,
if Israel would follow my ways,
NIV.14:
how quickly would I subdue their enemies
and turn my hand against their foes!
NIV.15:
Those who hate the Lord would cringe before him,
and their punishment would last forever.
NIV.16:
But you would be fed with the finest of wheat;
with honey from the rock I would satisfy you.”

NRSVCE.1:
Sing aloud to God our strength;
shout for joy to the God of Jacob.
NRSVCE.2:
Raise a song, sound the tambourine,
the sweet lyre with the harp.
NRSVCE.3:
Blow the trumpet at the new moon,
at the full moon, on our festal day.
NRSVCE.4:
For it is a statute for Israel,
an ordinance of the God of Jacob.
NRSVCE.5:
He made it a decree in Joseph,
when he went out over the land of Egypt.
I hear a voice I had not known:
NRSVCE.6:
“I relieved your shoulder of the burden;
your hands were freed from the basket.
NRSVCE.7:
In distress you called, and I rescued you;
I answered you in the secret place of thunder;
I tested you at the waters of Meribah. Selah
NRSVCE.8:
Hear, O my people, while I admonish you;
O Israel, if you would but listen to me!
NRSVCE.9:
There shall be no strange god among you;
you shall not bow down to a foreign god.
NRSVCE.10:
I am the LORD your God,
who brought you up out of the land of Egypt.
Open your mouth wide and I will fill it.
NRSVCE.11:
“But my people did not listen to my voice;
Israel would not submit to me.
NRSVCE.12:
So I gave them over to their stubborn hearts,
to follow their own counsels.
NRSVCE.13:
O that my people would listen to me,
that Israel would walk in my ways!
NRSVCE.14:
Then I would quickly subdue their enemies,
and turn my hand against their foes.
NRSVCE.15:
Those who hate the LORD would cringe before him,
and their doom would last forever.
NRSVCE.16:
I would feed you with the finest of the wheat,
and with honey from the rock I would satisfy you.”

NET.1: Shout for joy to God, our source of strength! Shout out to the God of Jacob!
NET.2: Sing a song and play the tambourine, the pleasant sounding harp, and the ten-stringed instrument!
NET.3: Sound the ram’s horn on the day of the new moon, and on the day of the full moon when our festival begins.
NET.4: For observing the festival is a requirement for Israel; it is an ordinance given by the God of Jacob.
NET.5: He decreed it as a regulation in Joseph, when he attacked the land of Egypt. I heard a voice I did not recognize.
NET.6: It said: “I removed the burden from his shoulder; his hands were released from holding the basket.
NET.7: In your distress you called out and I rescued you. I answered you from a dark thundercloud. I tested you at the waters of Meribah. (Selah)
NET.8: I said, ‘Listen, my people! I will warn you! O Israel, if only you would obey me!
NET.9: There must be no other god among you. You must not worship a foreign god.
NET.10: I am the LORD, your God, the one who brought you out of the land of Egypt. Open your mouth wide and I will fill it!’
NET.11: But my people did not obey me; Israel did not submit to me.
NET.12: I gave them over to their stubborn desires; they did what seemed right to them.
NET.13: If only my people would obey me! If only Israel would keep my commands!
NET.14: Then I would quickly subdue their enemies, and attack their adversaries.”
NET.15: (May those who hate the LORD cower in fear before him! May they be permanently humiliated!)
NET.16: “I would feed Israel the best wheat, and would satisfy your appetite with honey from the rocky cliffs.”
